title: TriangleMesh Object
description: The TriangleMesh object represents all of the data defining a triangular mesh.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: nodeCoordinates
properties.description: Returns the node coordinates as an array of Point3D objects.

properties.name: nodeCoordinatesAsDouble
properties.description: Returns the node coordinates as an array of doubles where they are the x, y, z components of each coordinate.

properties.name: nodeCoordinatesAsFloat
properties.description: Returns the node coordinates as an array of floats where they are the x, y, z components of each coordinate.

properties.name: nodeCount
properties.description: Returns the total number of nodes in the mesh.

properties.name: nodeIndices
properties.description: Returns an array of indices that define which nodes are used for each triangle. This is used to look-up the coordinates in the NodeCoordinates array to get the three coordinates of each triangle.

properties.name: normalVectors
properties.description: Returns the normal vectors of the mesh where there is a normal vector at each node. The normals are returned as an array of Vector3D objects.

properties.name: normalVectorsAsDouble
properties.description: Returns the normal vectors of the mesh where there is a normal vector at each node. The normals are returned as an array of doubles where they are the x, y, z components of each vector.

properties.name: normalVectorsAsFloat
properties.description: Returns the normal vectors of the mesh where there is a normal vector at each node. The normals are returned as an array of floats where they are the x, y, z components of each vector.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: surfaceTolerance
properties.description: Returns the surface tolerance that was used to generate this mesh. This is most useful when using display meshes that have already been calculated.

properties.name: textureCoordinates
properties.description: Returns the texture coordinates used when mapping a texture to this face. The coordinates are returned as an array of Point2D objects where the x and y properties of the point are u and v coordinates as defined in parametric space. There is a texture coordinate for each vertex in the face mesh.

properties.name: textureCoordinatesAsDouble
properties.description: Returns the texture coordinates used when mapping a texture to this face. The coordinates are returned as an array of doubles where they are the u and v components of each coordinate as defined in parametric space. There is a texture coordinate for each vertex in the face mesh.

properties.name: textureCoordinatesAsFloat
properties.description: Returns the texture coordinates used when mapping a texture to this face. The coordinates are returned as an array of floats where they are the u and v components of each coordinate as defined in parametric space. There is a texture coordinate for each vertex in the face mesh.

properties.name: triangleCount
properties.description: Returns the number of triangles in the mesh.

classType.title: TriangleMesh.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

isValid.title: TriangleMesh.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = triangleMesh_var.isValid
